summary: DHH mentions regretting not getting a wireless network card for his new iBook. He highlights MetroStar, a Danish startup bringing wifi to cafés in Copenhagen, noting they have seven locations so far. He's excited that one spot near his favorite sandwich place at Nyhavn might be within range.
key_points: Early enthusiasm for wifi mobility and the iBook's wireless capabilities
MetroStar was an early Danish venture bringing public wifi to cafés
Copenhagen's Nyhavn area was among the first wifi-enabled spots
money_quotes: I'm starting to regret that I didn't get my new iBook fitted with a wireless network card right from the get-go.
key_insight: A brief, early-blog-era post capturing the novelty of public wifi access in Copenhagen circa 2002.
